region_of_origin: United States (Subway global chain, Singapore outlet)
food_subgroup: Catering sandwich platter — assorted filled submarine rolls

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a mixed catering sandwich platter. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (Sandwiches) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a mixed multi-filling catering platter format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 (Sandwich) is the closest available concept. No specific code for a mixed catering platter exists in SNOMED CT.

main_ingredients: white or wheat submarine roll bread

serving.description: 1 portion (4-inch sub slice, approximately 1 of 15 portions from the platter)
serving.natural_language_portion: One realistic serving is one 4-inch sub slice (approximately 120 g), representing one of the 15 portions cut from five footlong submarine sandwiches in the catering platter.

nutrient_source.title: Subway Footlong Sandwich — composite nutrient estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central branded Subway entries: Italian BMT (FDC ID 2097634), Tuna (FDC ID 2097640), Cold Cut Combo (FDC ID 2097628), Oven Roasted Chicken (FDC ID 2097636), Teriyaki Chicken (FDC ID 2097642); values averaged per 100 g edible portion
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The Subway Flavour Craver Catering Platter is a Singapore-specific catering product comprising five Subway footlong varieties (Italian BMT, Tuna, Cold Cut Trio, Roasted Chicken Breast, Teriyaki Chicken) cut into 15 four-inch portions. No single USDA or HPB entry covers this exact platter. Nutrient values are estimated as the arithmetic mean of the five named Subway footlong varieties from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, converted to per-100 g basis. The five varieties contribute roughly equal portions to the platter, so equal weighting is applied. This is a reasonable approximation; actual values may vary by bread choice, condiments, and vegetable additions at point of preparation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of bread-based sandwiches — white and mixed-grain submarine bread analogue; reference food: white bread sandwich (GI ~53–55) per Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Subway Flavour Craver Catering Platter. GI is estimated at 53 based on the GI of white bread-based sandwiches (GI 53–55) from the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The platter uses standard Subway white or wheat rolls; the mixed protein and fat fillings (meats, tuna, chicken) moderately lower the effective GI relative to plain bread. GI 53 is a conservative lower-bound estimate consistent with published sandwich GI data. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 33.0) / 100 ≈ 17.5, rounded to 17.6 for a 120 g serving containing approximately 33 g carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; approximately 744 mg per 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this platter due to its high sodium content from processed deli meats, bread, and condiments; one 4-inch portion provides approximately 744 mg sodium, representing about one-third of the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board for hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~17.6 per serving) and refined carbohydrate content from white submarine bread
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful that each 4-inch portion contains approximately 33 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~17.6; choosing whole-grain bread options and limiting high-sugar condiments such as teriyaki sauce can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from processed deli meats (salami, pepperoni, bologna) and sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portions of this platter due to its saturated fat content (~2.1 g per 100 g) from processed meats and its high sodium density; selecting leaner filling varieties such as roasted chicken breast and avoiding high-fat condiments can reduce cardiovascular risk per serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Subway Flavour Craver Catering Platter is a Singapore-market catering product offered by Subway restaurants, designed for group meals at meetings, corporate events, and social gatherings. The platter consists of five full-length (12-inch / footlong) Subway submarine sandwiches, each representing a different signature filling variety: Italian B.M.T. (salami, pepperoni, and ham), Tuna (tuna salad with mayonnaise), Cold Cut Trio (bologna, salami, and ham), Roasted Chicken Breast (oven-roasted chicken slices), and Teriyaki Chicken (sweet teriyaki-glazed chicken strips). Each footlong is pre-cut into three 4-inch portions, yielding 15 individual portions per platter, suitable for 10 to 15 people. The sandwiches are assembled on Subway's standard white or wheat Italian-style bread rolls and typically include fresh vegetable toppings such as lettuce, tomato, cucumber, onion, green pepper, and olives, with condiments applied at preparation. Nutritionally, each 4-inch portion provides approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content (~12.5 g/100 g) from the varied meat and fish fillings, moderate carbohydrate (~27.5 g/100 g) primarily from the bread, and a relatively low fat content (~6.8 g/100 g). The platter is notably high in sodium (~620 mg/100 g) due to the processed deli meats and bread, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low GI category) based on analogous white bread sandwich data, with a glycemic load of approximately 17.6 per serving, placing it in the moderate glycemic load range. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size and bread choice; those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease should be aware of the sodium and saturated fat contributions from processed meat fillings. The platter is available through field survey data and is served at Subway outlets including Subway Funan.

Subway catering platter field survey data
Subway catering platter for meetings Singapore

confidence_reason: The Subway Flavour Craver Catering Platter is a Singapore-specific catering product with no direct entry in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a platter unit. Nutrient values are estimated as the arithmetic mean of the five named Subway footlong varieties available in USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. This is a reasonable and defensible approximation but is not an exact match. Confidence is therefore medium. GI is estimated from analogous white bread sandwich data from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated as the arithmetic mean of five Subway footlong varieties (Italian BMT, Tuna, Cold Cut Trio, Oven Roasted Chicken, Teriyaki Chicken) from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, converted to per-100 g basis. Actual values will vary depending on bread type, condiment choices, and vegetable additions at point of preparation. 2. `vitamin_d_mcg` is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this composite estimate. 3. `vitamin_e_mg` is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this composite estimate. 4. `selenium_mcg` is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this composite estimate. 5. The halal status reflects Subway Singapore's halal certification from MUIS (Majlis Ugama Islam Singapura); individual outlet certification should be verified. 6. GI is estimated at 53 based on analogous white bread sandwich data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283. 7. The platter price is SGD 75.00 as listed on field survey data. 8. Serving size of 120 g represents one 4-inch portion (1 of 15 cuts from 5 footlongs); the full platter weighs approximately 1,800 g. 9. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from available USDA branded Subway entries and should be treated as approximate.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified need to verify aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list and document -1 sentinel values more explicitly.

Iteration 2: Corrected g3_numeric_hygiene by refining the documentation for `vitamin_d_mcg`, `vitamin_e_mg`, and `selenium_mcg` in the `notes` field to be more direct and specific to the source. Corrected q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_coverage by adding the missing traditional Chinese alias '賽百味風味拼盤外賣餐盤' and the romanized alias 'Sài Bǎi Wèi Fēng Wèi Pīn Pán' to the flat `aliases` list, ensuring perfect consistency with `aliases_by_language`. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass. Decision: PASS.
